courseTitle: GitOps for a Student Course Portal
headline: Version 1.1.0 adds a richer release brief and tighter automated feedback for students
summary: This example demonstrates a stronger GitOps classroom flow: a more useful page, faster upstream test feedback, and a production page that clearly explains what changed after merge.

changeRequest.title: Publish lesson 5 and reduce release friction
changeRequest.description: Add the next lesson module, shorten manual review time, and keep GitHub Pages deployments transparent for students following the release.

pipelineStages.name: Unit Tests
pipelineStages.purpose: Catch upstream logic regressions in string processing and rollout scoring.

pipelineStages.name: Integration Tests
pipelineStages.purpose: Verify the built artifact exposes the data that the frontend actually renders.

pipelineStages.name: Preview Review
pipelineStages.purpose: Review the richer teaching page before the change reaches main.

pipelineStages.name: GitHub Pages Deploy
pipelineStages.purpose: Publish the merged state and stamp the page with the latest deployment metadata.

learningPath: Create a feature branch from main to prepare release 1.1.0.
Submit a pull request so CI can validate the desired state and score the rollout.
Use the unit-test and integration-test results to review the release with confidence.
Merge into main so CD rebuilds the site and writes the new deployment metadata.
Let GitHub Pages publish the update so students can see the new lesson and release brief.
releaseNotes: The new release brief explains the business change, owners, and rollout posture in plain language.
The pipeline is faster because the unit-test layer catches upstream logic issues earlier.
Students can compare the new version, commit hash, and updated timestamp after deployment.
studentQuestions: How does faster unit-test feedback change the way you review a pull request?
Which deployment metric on the page would you watch first after the merge?
What evidence on the live page proves that GitHub Pages deployed the merged commit?
